perturbation.original_text: All sponsorship and advertising package deals for the Co-Branded Site will be mutually agreed upon by the parties and shall abide by MediaLinx's and HCI's standard advertising agreement and Canadian and U.S. advertising regulations and standards. Any excess inventory that has not been sold is available to be sold by either party. Neither party shall permit advertising on the Co-Branded Site from an entity which is a competitor to the other party. For the purposes of this Agreement, a "competitor" to MediaLinx shall be an Internet portal web site and/or an internet service provider. For the purposes of this Agreement, a "competitor" to HCI shall be a consumer health based web site. The parties shall define a plan in writing prior to the Launch setting out how the Parties shall work together to jointly sell advertising, serve the advertising and track the advertising inventory on the Co-Branded Site.
perturbation.changed_text: All sponsorship and advertising package deals for the Co-Branded Site will be mutually agreed upon by the parties and shall abide by MediaLinx's and HCI's standard advertising agreement and Canadian and U.S. advertising regulations and standards. Any excess inventory that has not been sold is available to be sold by either party. Either party shall permit advertising on the Co-Branded Site from an entity which is a competitor to the other party. For the purposes of this Agreement, a "competitor" to MediaLinx shall be an Internet portal web site and/or an internet service provider. For the purposes of this Agreement, a "competitor" to HCI shall be a consumer health based web site. The parties shall define a plan in writing prior to the Launch setting out how the Parties shall work together to jointly sell advertising, serve the advertising and track the advertising inventory on the Co-Branded Site.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states 'Neither party shall permit advertising on the Co-Branded Site from an entity which is a competitor to the other party'. The modified text changes this to 'Either party shall permit advertising on the Co-Branded Site from an entity which is a competitor to the other party'. This creates a direct contradiction regarding whether competitors can advertise on the Co-Branded Site, which is a conflict within Section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to paragraph 6, MediaLinx shall be entitled to sell advertising and sponsorships on all pages of the Co-Branded Site to Canadian advertisers (for the purposes of this Agreement "Canadian advertisers" shall be advertisers with substantial places of business in Canada) and HCI shall be entitled to sell advertising and sponsorships on all pages of the Co-Branded Site to US advertisers (for the purposes of this Agreement, US advertisers shall be advertisers with substantial places of business in the United States). All sponsorship and advertising package deals for the Co-Branded Site will be mutually agreed upon by the parties and shall abide by MediaLinx's and HCI's standard advertising agreement and Canadian and U.S. advertising regulations and standards. Any excess inventory that has not been sold is available to be sold by either party. Either party shall permit advertising on the Co-Branded Site from an entity which is a competitor to the other party. For the purposes of this Agreement, a "competitor" to MediaLinx shall be an Internet portal web site and/or an internet service provider. For the purposes of this Agreement, a "competitor" to HCI shall be a consumer health based web site. The parties shall define a plan in writing prior to the Launch setting out how the Parties shall work together to jointly sell advertising, serve the advertising and track the advertising inventory on the Co-Branded Site.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that 'Neither party shall permit advertising on the Co-Branded Site from an entity which is a competitor to the other party.' The changed text modifies this to 'Either party shall permit advertising on the Co-Branded Site from an entity which is a competitor to the other party.' This creates a direct and irreconcilable contradiction within the same section, rendering the agreement unclear and potentially unenforceable regarding advertising by competitors. The contradicted text is contained within the same section, where the changed text fundamentally alters a key restriction.

perturbation.original_text: 11. TERM & TERMINATION 11.1 Term The term of this Agreement shall be for a period of two (2) years (the "Term"). The effective date of the start of the Term will be from the Launch Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. TERM & TERMINATION 11.1 Term The term of this Agreement shall be for a period of ten (10) years (the "Term"). The effective date of the start of the Term will be from the Launch Date.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term from 'two (2) years' to 'ten (10) years' creates a contradiction with other clauses referencing the original term. For example, the financial calculations and exclusivity clauses might be implicitly or explicitly tied to the initial two-year term, leading to disputes over the intended duration of these commitments. The contradiction primarily impacts how long the agreement is valid, since there are time dependent factors along the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: FINANCIALS (a) HCI shall pay MLX a guaranteed fixed sum of [*] US per annum during the Initial Term of this Agreement to be paid out as a monthly payment of [*] US (the "Monthly Payment"). This Monthly Payment shall be due by the 10th day of each month; (b) HCI shall be entitled to all net revenue generated from advertising and e- commerce transactions generated by Canadian companies that may take place on the Co-Branded Site up to and including [*] US in any given year. Thereafter, any net revenues exceeding [*] US shall be shared fifty-fifty between the parties (50% MediaLinx--50% HCI). HCI shall be entitled to all proceeds generated from US companies on the Co-Branded Site. "Net revenue" is defined as revenue less: 1) third party expenses directly related to the acquisition of the revenue; for example, advertising broker commissions and advertising management services, and 2) internally paid commissions for selling the advertisement and/or sponsorship; (c) Both Parties shall keep true and accurate books and records of all revenue generated as a result of the Co-Branded Site. Either Party shall have the right from time to time to audit and make extracts of the books and records of the other, insofar as said books or records pertain to the terms of this Agreement. Any such audit shall take place upon not less than seven (7) days advance written notice, during normal business hours of normal business days and at reasonable intervals. Any such audit shall be at the Source: HEALTHCENTRAL COM, S-1/A, 11/8/1999 requesting Party's expense, unless the audit determines that the other party has underpaid by 10% or more. In the event that the calculation of payments herein is determined by a computer-based system, then the "books and records" shall include, without limitation, the machine-sensible data (e.g. punch cards, magnetic tapes, discs; and (d) Both Parties shall provide the other with quarterly accounting reports as necessary. The quarterly reporting periods end September 30th and December 31st, 1999, March 31st, 2000, and June 30th, 2000. Applicable remittances and accounting reports will follow under separate cover within sixty (60) days of the end of each quarter.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'two (2) years' to 'ten (10) years' directly impacts the 'Initial Term' referenced in the FINANCIALS section. Specifically, the payments outlined in section (a) are guaranteed for the 'Initial Term'. Changing the term's duration means that the fixed payments originally calculated for a two-year period would now apply to a ten-year period, fundamentally altering the financial obligations of the parties. This creates a clear contradiction because the financial calculations and expectations were based on the original two-year agreement.

perturbation.original_text: MediaLinx will not have access to the HCI Personal Health Records (being the longitudinal repository of personal health information about users of the Co-Branded Site) during or after the Term of this Agreement. During the Term of this Agreement and for a period of six (6) months thereafter, MediaLinx shall not use or publicly disclose the data collected from users of the Co-Branded Site if such use is for the purpose of providing health information, advertisements, or products that compete with those that reside on the Co-Branded Site. In other instances, MediaLinx shall be entitled to use or publicly disclose the data collected subject to privacy guidelines noted above. In addition to the foregoing, HCI shall ensure that the Co-Branded Site is integrated into the Sympatico site-wide registry in order for users to have a single login access for all Sympatico.ca and Sympatico co-branded web sites.
perturbation.changed_text: MediaLinx will not have access to the HCI Personal Health Records (being the longitudinal repository of personal health information about users of the Co-Branded Site) during the Term of this Agreement, but they will have access to it, after the Term of this Agreement. During the Term of this Agreement and for a period of six (6) months thereafter, MediaLinx shall not use or publicly disclose the data collected from users of the Co-Branded Site if such use is for the purpose of providing health information, advertisements, or products that compete with those that reside on the Co-Branded Site. In other instances, MediaLinx shall be entitled to use or publicly disclose the data collected subject to privacy guidelines noted above. In addition to the foregoing, HCI shall ensure that the Co-Branded Site is integrated into the Sympatico site-wide registry in order for users to have a single login access for all Sympatico.ca and Sympatico co-branded web sites.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that MediaLinx will not have access to HCI Personal Health Records 'during or after the Term of this Agreement'. The modified text changes this to 'during the Term of this Agreement, but they will have access to it, after the Term of this Agreement'. This creates a direct contradiction regarding whether or not MediaLinx will have access to HCI Personal Health Records, after the term, which is a conflict within Section 7. It's also contradicting the sentence right after this perturbation, since it is referring the term of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CUSTOMER INFORMATION Any and all customer data that is collected from the Co-Branded Site shall be collected and used in accordance with Canadian federal and provincial laws and U.S. Federal and State laws and both MediaLinx and HCI privacy guidelines and MediaLinx and HCI shall each own an undivided interest in such data. MediaLinx will not have access to the HCI Personal Health Records (being the longitudinal repository of personal health information about users of the Co-Branded Site) during the Term of this Agreement, but they will have access to it, after the Term of this Agreement. During the Term of this Agreement and for a period of six (6) months thereafter, MediaLinx shall not use or publicly disclose the data collected from users of the Co-Branded Site if such use is for the purpose of providing health information, advertisements, or products that compete with those that reside on the Co-Branded Site. In other instances, MediaLinx shall be entitled to use or publicly disclose the data collected subject to privacy guidelines noted above. In addition to the foregoing, HCI shall ensure that the Co-Branded Site is integrated into the Sympatico site-wide registry in order for users to have a single login access for all Sympatico.ca and Sympatico co-branded web sites.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The original text states MediaLinx will not have access 'during or after' the term, while the changed text specifies no access 'during', but access 'after' the term. The contradicted text is the changed text, and the changed text is directly contradicting itself in the same section.
